habitat: Hilly/montane open forests; forest edge; scrubby edge; valleys; meadows; and coniferous forest
story: Meet the Hodgsons Redstart (Phoenicurus hodgsoni), a distinctive bird from the Muscicapidae family. Preferring habitats such as Hilly/montane open forests; forest edge; scrubby edge; valleys; meadows; and coniferous forest, this species has adapted to thrive under specific environmental conditions. With an IUCN status of Least Concern (LC), its population trends are closely watched by scientists and enthusiasts. The Hodgsons Redstart serves as an indicator species for the health of its environment. Whether you are a seasoned ornithologist or a curious nature lover, learning about the Hodgsons Redstart enriches our understanding of nature.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
